quote: All of humanity’s problems stem from man’s inability to sit quietly in a room alone.
through_line: What runs through Thursday is patience — the discipline of looking past the immediate signal. The AI labour debate, it turns out, is still in the first inning: aggregate data is inconclusive, and the real effects are uneven, slow-moving, and shaped by institutions more than algorithms. Soft skills quietly outperformed technical skills in 2025 — not dramatically, but measurably. Non-dual awareness is beginning to yield to scientific study, not despite its subtlety but because of it. And interoception — the body’s hidden sixth sense — is drawing serious research investment precisely because we still barely understand it. Careful attention, it turns out, is itself a research method.

articles.title: Research on AI and the labor market is still in the first inning
articles.pitch: The Peterson Institute for International Economics makes a careful case that the empirical picture of AI’s labour impact remains genuinely murky — not because nothing is happening, but because the observation windows are too short and confounding factors too dense to isolate AI’s contribution. A useful corrective to both the catastrophists and the reassurers.

articles.title: WordPress in 2026: traditional, headless, static or hybrid?
articles.pitch: For practitioners not ready to fully abandon WordPress but feeling the pull of modern architectures, this piece maps the middle ground: what headless WordPress actually looks like in practice, where the hybrid approach makes sense, and which path fits which kind of project. Practical and non-dogmatic.

articles.title: Frontend Development Trends 2026: AI, Edge & TypeScript
articles.pitch: A read on where the frontend stack is settling in 2026 — the dominance of TypeScript, the maturation of edge rendering, and AI’s growing role as a development co-pilot rather than a replacement. Less a trends list and more an attempt to identify which shifts are structural and which are noise.

articles.title: The 2025 Education Shift: Why Soft Skills Outperformed Technical Skills This Year
articles.pitch: For the first time, communication, leadership, and collaboration topped LinkedIn’s hardest-to-hire-for skills list — surpassing technical proficiencies. This piece examines what drove the shift and what it means for educators designing curricula that will actually serve students in the world they’re entering.

articles.title: Non-Dual Awareness & Science
articles.pitch: A substantive look at how nondual awareness — the dissolution of subject/object duality at the heart of Advaita Vedanta and Dzogchen — is beginning to be studied scientifically, including its neural correlates and its relationship to wellbeing. Engages the epistemological complications honestly rather than glossing them.

articles.title: Scientists launch $14.2 million project to map the body’s ‘hidden sixth sense’
articles.pitch: Interoception — the brain’s ongoing read of signals from inside the body — shapes emotion, decision-making, and sense of self in ways that are only beginning to be understood. A major new research initiative aims to create the first comprehensive atlas of the body-brain signalling system, treating it as the frontier of neuroscience it arguably already is.
